refined_fr.refined_fr: Capture and represent worksite spatial features in a machine-readable model.
refined_fr.rationale: The candidate FR bundles three distinct obligations: (1) terrain analysis, (2) obstacle identification, and (3) work-zone representation. Each is a separate functional concern with different data sources, processing logic, and stakeholder consumption patterns. The refined FR is solution-neutral (avoids 'mapping/modeling' terminology which implies implementation approach) and focuses on the core problem: making worksite geometry and features available in a structured, queryable form. Splitting into atomic sub-FRs allows independent DP selection and IM packaging.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Confirm that a worksite model exists in a defined schema/format, contains spatial coordinates and feature classifications, and is accessible to downstream planning and execution functions per the acceptance criteria.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-006.1: Acquire terrain elevation and surface geometry data from the worksite.
FR-NGHE-006.2: Identify and classify static obstacles (structures, vegetation, utilities) within the worksite boundary.
FR-NGHE-006.3: Define and represent work-zone boundaries and operational areas within the worksite model.
FR-NGHE-006.4: Integrate terrain, obstacle, and work-zone data into a unified spatial model accessible to planning and execution functions.
refined_fr.note: The refined FR is atomic at the integration level; the sub-FRs are the truly atomic functional requirements that should each map to distinct DPs and IMs.

candidate_dps: DP1: Acquire spatial feature data through multi-modal sensing (vision, range, inertial) and fuse into a unified coordinate frame; store as a structured geometric model (point clouds, meshes, or voxel grids) with semantic labels and metadata; expose via query interface for downstream planning/execution consumers.

DP2: Decompose worksite into discrete zones; assign each zone a feature inventory (obstacles, hazards, access points) via manual annotation or semi-automated detection; represent as a graph of zone nodes with edge constraints; persist in a queryable schema.

DP3: Capture worksite features as a rule-based symbolic representation (e.g., logic rules or ontology) that encodes spatial relationships, safety constraints, and operational policies; derive a queryable model on demand by evaluating rules against sensor observations.

DP4: Establish a real-time streaming pipeline that continuously ingests sensor data, applies feature extraction and classification, and maintains an incrementally updated spatial model; expose model state via subscription/polling interface for planning/execution.

DP5: Delegate feature capture to a hybrid approach: use pre-defined templates (e.g., site layout blueprints, CAD models) as a baseline; augment with live sensor observations to detect deviations, dynamic hazards, and unplanned features; merge into a composite model.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP5: Hybrid baseline-augmented model. Establish a composite spatial representation by anchoring to pre-defined worksite templates (blueprints, CAD, or prior maps) and augmenting with live multi-modal sensor observations to detect deviations, dynamic hazards, and unplanned features. Fuse baseline and live data into a unified, queryable geometric and semantic model. Expose model state and feature queries via a standardized interface for planning and execution consumers. Maintain model freshness and consistency through incremental updates and conflict resolution policies aligned with safety and operational constraints.

potential_sources: Tight coupling between sensor modality selection and feature extraction logic—mitigated by abstracting sensor input as a generic observation stream and keeping extraction logic modular.

Dependency on baseline template availability and accuracy—mitigated by graceful degradation: if baseline unavailable, fall back to DP1 (pure sensor fusion); if baseline inaccurate, detection of deviations triggers re-calibration.
Coupling between model update frequency and planning/execution cycle time—mitigated by decoupling via asynchronous query interface; consumers poll or subscribe at their own cadence.
Potential coupling to specific safety/operational policy encoding—mitigated by externalizing policies as queryable constraints rather than hard-coding into model structure.
coupling_check.notes: DP5 achieves loose coupling by separating concerns: baseline provision (external), live sensing (pluggable), fusion logic (centralized), and policy enforcement (declarative). The hybrid approach reduces brittleness compared to pure sensor fusion (DP1) or pure symbolic reasoning (DP3), and supports incremental model refinement without full re-acquisition.

assumptions_risks: ASSUMPTION: Worksite baseline (blueprint, CAD, or prior map) is available and reasonably accurate at mission start. RISK: If baseline is stale, missing, or significantly inaccurate, fusion may produce misleading composite model. MITIGATION: Implement baseline validation checks; flag high-confidence deviations; support manual override.

ASSUMPTION: Multi-modal sensor suite (vision, range, inertial) is available and calibrated. RISK: Sensor failures, miscalibration, or occlusions degrade feature detection. MITIGATION: Implement sensor health monitoring; use redundancy where safety-critical; graceful degradation to available modalities.

ASSUMPTION: Feature extraction and semantic labeling can be automated or semi-automated. RISK: Misclassification of hazards or features leads to unsafe planning. MITIGATION: Conservative labeling (over-report hazards); human-in-the-loop validation for high-risk features; traceability of labeling confidence.

ASSUMPTION: Worksite safety plan and operational policies are formalized and queryable. RISK: Policies are ambiguous, incomplete, or conflict with model representation. MITIGATION: Explicit policy schema; validation against model; escalation for unresolvable conflicts.

ASSUMPTION: Planning and execution functions can tolerate model latency and eventual consistency. RISK: Stale model leads to unsafe or inefficient decisions. MITIGATION: Define acceptable model age; implement update prioritization; alert consumers to model freshness.

ASSUMPTION: Composite model can be efficiently queried and updated in real-time. RISK: Model complexity or update frequency exceeds computational budget. MITIGATION: Hierarchical model representation; lazy evaluation; caching; spatial indexing.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP5 to explicitly separate three sub-concerns: (1) Baseline provisioning and validation (DP5a), (2) Live feature detection and anomaly flagging (DP5b), and (3) Fusion, conflict resolution, and query interface (DP5c). This decomposition clarifies ownership boundaries (IM layer) and allows independent evolution of baseline sources, sensor pipelines, and model consumers. Additionally, introduce a formal model schema (e.g., GeoJSON + semantic tags + policy constraints) to ensure interoperability and auditability. Define acceptance criteria for model freshness, feature detection recall/precision, and policy compliance.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Centralized spatial representation engine that maintains hybrid baseline-augmented models, manages sensor fusion, handles conflict resolution, and exposes queryable geometric/semantic state to planning and execution consumers.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Ingests live multi-modal sensor observations (LiDAR, camera, IMU, GPS) from Vehicle and Teleoperation to augment baseline model and detect deviations/hazards.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Exposes queryable model state and feature queries (geometric and semantic) to planning and execution consumers for mission planning and real-time decision-making.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Publishes detected deviations, dynamic hazards, and unplanned features to Safety Services for constraint-aligned conflict resolution and safety enforcement.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Receives baseline templates (blueprints, CAD, prior maps) and synchronizes incremental model updates across distributed nodes to maintain freshness and consistency.

module_containment_scores.notes: Excellent containment. NGHE Solution is the natural home for spatial representation, sensor fusion, and model management. Baseline template ingestion, live sensor augmentation, conflict resolution, and queryable state exposure all align with a centralized geospatial/semantic engine. Minimal diffusion: only telemetry ingestion (Vehicle/Teleoperation) and query consumption (planning/execution) cross boundaries via standard interfaces.

Poor containment. Vehicle is a platform/agent, not a representation engine. Placing hybrid model logic here would scatter sensor fusion, baseline anchoring, and conflict resolution across vehicle instances, causing severe duplication and inconsistency. Model state would diffuse to Teleoperation, Safety Services, and planning systems.

Poor containment. Teleoperation is a control/command interface, not a persistent spatial representation layer. Embedding baseline-augmented model logic here would couple representation state to operator sessions, break model persistence, and force duplication across multiple teleoperation instances. High diffusion to Vehicle, NGHE Solution, and Safety Services.

Poor containment. Safety Services is a constraint enforcement and alerting layer, not a spatial representation engine. While Safety Services consumes model queries and enforces conflict resolution policies, it should not own the hybrid model itself. Placing representation logic here would couple safety logic to geometric/semantic fusion, violating separation of concerns. High diffusion to NGHE Solution, Vehicle, and planning systems.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: NGHE Solution is the single authoritative owner of the hybrid baseline-augmented spatial model. It ingests baseline templates via Network Link, fuses live multi-modal telemetry, maintains geometric and semantic state, detects deviations and hazards, and exposes queryable model state via Tasking. Safety Services consumes model queries and enforces conflict resolution policies; Vehicle and Teleoperation provide sensor observations; planning and execution systems query the model for decision-making. This architecture centralizes representation logic, eliminates duplication, and ensures model consistency and freshness.
containment_risks: NGHE Solution may become a bottleneck if model update latency or query throughput is insufficient for real-time planning and safety-critical decision-making. Mitigation: implement incremental update batching, caching, and asynchronous query patterns.

Conflict resolution policies must be clearly defined and aligned with safety constraints. If policies are ambiguous or inconsistent, Safety Services and planning systems may diverge in their interpretation of model state. Mitigation: formalize conflict resolution rules as part of the model schema and expose them via Tasking interface.

Baseline template ingestion and versioning must be robust. If baseline updates are not properly synchronized across distributed nodes, model consistency will degrade. Mitigation: implement version control, rollback, and audit logging for baseline templates.

Multi-modal sensor fusion requires careful calibration and validation. If sensor observations are noisy, misaligned, or conflicting, augmented model state may be unreliable. Mitigation: implement sensor validation, outlier detection, and confidence scoring in the fusion pipeline.

Queryable interface must be well-specified and versioned to avoid breaking changes for planning and execution consumers. Mitigation: define a stable, extensible query schema and maintain backward compatibility.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: High. The FR 'Capture and represent worksite spatial features in a machine-readable model' is directly verifiable against acceptance criteria: (1) worksite model/map generated and available to planning/execution functions for agreed site features, and (2) model alignment with worksite safety plan and operational policies. NGHE Solution as IM owner provides a single, queryable source of truth. Verification can measure model completeness (feature coverage), freshness (update latency), consistency (baseline-live fusion accuracy), and safety constraint compliance (conflict resolution alignment).
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Excellent. The FR->DP->IM chain is clear: FR defines the problem (spatial feature capture and representation); DP specifies the solution approach (hybrid baseline-augmented model with sensor fusion, conflict resolution, and queryable interface); IM allocates ownership to NGHE Solution with well-defined external interfaces (Telemetry in, Tasking out, SafetyAlert out, Network Link inout). Ownership boundaries are unambiguous, and interface contracts are explicit.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP5 (Hybrid baseline-augmented model) is a sound, complete solution to the FR. It addresses all functional requirements: (1) baseline anchoring (pre-defined templates), (2) live augmentation (multi-modal sensor fusion), (3) deviation/hazard detection, (4) unified geometric/semantic representation, (5) queryable interface for consumers, and (6) freshness/consistency maintenance. The DP is technology-agnostic, scalable, and aligned with safety and operational constraints. No logical gaps or contradictions detected.

diffusion_risks: Telemetry ingestion (Vehicle/Teleoperation -> NGHE Solution): Risk of sensor data quality degradation if Vehicle or Teleoperation pre-processes or filters observations before transmission. Mitigation: define raw sensor data contracts and validate at NGHE Solution ingestion boundary.

Tasking interface (NGHE Solution -> planning/execution): Risk of query interface fragmentation if planning and execution systems evolve independently and demand incompatible query schemas. Mitigation: version the Tasking interface, maintain backward compatibility, and establish a query schema governance process.

SafetyAlert interface (NGHE Solution -> Safety Services): Risk of conflict resolution policy divergence if Safety Services interprets model deviations differently than NGHE Solution. Mitigation: formalize conflict resolution rules in the model schema and expose them via SafetyAlert or a dedicated policy interface.

Network Link (baseline template synchronization): Risk of model inconsistency if baseline updates are not atomically synchronized across distributed NGHE Solution instances. Mitigation: implement version control, distributed consensus, and audit logging for baseline ingestion.

Bottleneck risk: NGHE Solution may become a performance bottleneck if model update latency or query throughput cannot meet real-time planning and safety-critical decision-making demands. Mitigation: implement incremental batching, caching, read replicas, and asynchronous query patterns.

Sensor fusion complexity: Risk of augmented model unreliability if multi-modal sensor observations are noisy, misaligned, or conflicting. Mitigation: implement sensor validation, outlier detection, confidence scoring, and fusion algorithm validation against ground truth.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Excellent containment (0.92 score). NGHE Solution is the natural, single authoritative owner of the hybrid baseline-augmented spatial model. All core DP responsibilities (baseline anchoring, live sensor augmentation, conflict resolution, queryable state exposure) are co-located within NGHE Solution. Minimal diffusion: only telemetry ingestion (Vehicle/Teleoperation) and query consumption (planning/execution/Safety Services) cross boundaries via standard, well-defined interfaces. Alternative allocations (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) would scatter representation logic, cause severe duplication, and violate separation of concerns. No recommended re-allocation.

recommended_revisions: Formalize conflict resolution policies as part of the model schema (e.g., priority rules for baseline vs. live data, temporal decay for sensor observations, safety constraint overrides). Expose policies via Tasking interface or a dedicated policy query endpoint to ensure Safety Services and planning systems interpret model state consistently.

Define and version the Tasking query interface (schema, query language, response format) as a stable, extensible contract. Establish governance for backward-compatible evolution to prevent breaking changes for planning and execution consumers.

Specify sensor data contracts for Telemetry interface: raw data formats, calibration metadata, confidence/quality scores, and timestamp semantics. Validate ingested telemetry at NGHE Solution boundary to detect and reject malformed or out-of-spec observations.

Implement baseline template versioning and synchronization protocol for Network Link: atomic ingestion, rollback capability, audit logging, and distributed consensus for multi-node NGHE Solution deployments. Define baseline update frequency and latency SLAs.

Establish model freshness and consistency SLAs: maximum update latency for live sensor augmentation, query response time bounds, and consistency guarantees (eventual vs. strong) for distributed NGHE Solution instances. Measure and monitor against these SLAs.

Design sensor fusion pipeline with validation, outlier detection, and confidence scoring. Validate fusion accuracy against ground truth (e.g., manual site surveys, high-fidelity reference maps) during commissioning and periodically during operation.

Define model query performance requirements (throughput, latency percentiles) and implement caching, read replicas, or asynchronous query patterns to prevent NGHE Solution from becoming a bottleneck for real-time planning and safety-critical decision-making.

Establish acceptance test criteria for model completeness: coverage of agreed site features, accuracy of geometric and semantic representation, detection sensitivity for deviations and hazards, and alignment with worksite safety plan and operational policies.

decision.justification: All three gates (FR-IM, FR-DP, DP-IM) pass with high confidence and excellent containment (0.92). The FR is clear, the DP is sound and complete, and the IM allocation to NGHE Solution is unambiguous with well-defined interfaces. However, the recommended_revisions list identifies critical sub-problems that must be decomposed to operationalize the DP: (1) conflict resolution policy formalization and exposure, (2) Tasking query interface versioning and governance, (3) sensor data contract specification and validation, (4) baseline template versioning and synchronization, (5) model freshness/consistency SLAs, (6) sensor fusion pipeline validation, and (7) query performance optimization. These are not defects in the current FR-DP-IM chain but rather essential sub-functional requirements that emerge from the DP's complexity and the high safety criticality of the domain. Decomposing them now ensures that architectural and operational details are captured, owned, and verified before implementation. Stopping here would leave critical design decisions (e.g., conflict resolution semantics, query interface contracts, sensor fusion validation) unresolved and at risk of divergent interpretation during realization.
next_level_sub_frs: FR5.1: Formalize and expose conflict resolution policies (baseline vs. live data priority, temporal decay, safety constraint overrides) as queryable model metadata or dedicated policy interface to ensure consistent interpretation across Safety Services, planning, and execution systems.

FR5.2: Define, version, and govern the Tasking query interface (schema, query language, response format, backward compatibility) as a stable contract to prevent breaking changes for planning and execution consumers.

FR5.3: Specify and validate sensor data contracts for Telemetry ingestion (raw data formats, calibration metadata, confidence/quality scores, timestamp semantics) at NGHE Solution boundary to detect and reject malformed or out-of-spec observations.
FR5.4: Implement baseline template versioning, atomic synchronization, rollback capability, and audit logging for Network Link to ensure consistency across distributed NGHE Solution instances.

FR5.5: Establish and monitor model freshness and consistency SLAs (maximum update latency, query response time bounds, eventual vs. strong consistency guarantees) aligned with real-time planning and safety-critical decision-making demands.

FR5.6: Design and validate sensor fusion pipeline with outlier detection, confidence scoring, and ground-truth calibration to ensure augmented model reliability under noisy, misaligned, or conflicting multi-modal observations.

FR5.7: Optimize model query performance (throughput, latency percentiles) via caching, read replicas, or asynchronous patterns to prevent NGHE Solution from becoming a bottleneck for real-time planning and safety-critical decision-making.

revise.why: No revisions to current FR, DP, or IM are required. All gates pass and recommended_revisions are sub-functional decompositions, not corrections to the current level.

assumptions: NGHE Solution has sufficient computational and I/O capacity to ingest multi-modal telemetry, perform real-time sensor fusion, and serve queryable model state without exceeding latency or throughput SLAs.

Vehicle and Teleoperation systems can provide raw sensor data (or minimally pre-processed observations) with calibration metadata and confidence scores to enable NGHE Solution to perform robust fusion.
Planning and execution systems can adapt to a versioned, queryable Tasking interface and do not require synchronous, low-latency model updates for every decision cycle.
Baseline templates are available, maintainable, and can be synchronized across distributed NGHE Solution instances via Network Link with acceptable latency and consistency guarantees.

Safety Services can interpret conflict resolution policies and model deviations consistently with NGHE Solution's formalized rules, or policies can be exposed and synchronized via dedicated interface.
Ground-truth reference data (e.g., manual site surveys, high-fidelity maps) is available during commissioning and periodic validation to calibrate and verify sensor fusion accuracy.
open_risks: Sensor data quality degradation if Vehicle or Teleoperation pre-processes or filters observations before transmission to NGHE Solution. Mitigation: define raw sensor data contracts and validate at ingestion boundary.

Tasking query interface fragmentation if planning and execution systems evolve independently and demand incompatible schemas. Mitigation: version interface, maintain backward compatibility, establish governance.
Conflict resolution policy divergence if Safety Services interprets model deviations differently than NGHE Solution. Mitigation: formalize policies in model schema and expose via interface.
Baseline template synchronization inconsistency across distributed NGHE Solution instances. Mitigation: implement version control, distributed consensus, audit logging.

NGHE Solution performance bottleneck if model update latency or query throughput cannot meet real-time planning and safety-critical decision-making demands. Mitigation: implement caching, read replicas, asynchronous patterns.
Sensor fusion unreliability if multi-modal observations are noisy, misaligned, or conflicting. Mitigation: implement validation, outlier detection, confidence scoring, ground-truth calibration.

Model completeness gaps if agreed site features are not captured or represented with sufficient geometric/semantic fidelity. Mitigation: establish acceptance test criteria and measure coverage during commissioning.
evidence_needed: Formalized conflict resolution policy specification (priority rules, temporal decay functions, safety constraint override logic) with examples and test cases.
Tasking query interface specification (schema, query language, response format, versioning strategy, backward compatibility rules) with example queries and responses.
Sensor data contract specification (raw data formats, calibration metadata, confidence/quality score semantics, timestamp conventions) with validation rules and rejection criteria.
Baseline template versioning and synchronization protocol specification (atomic ingestion, rollback, audit logging, distributed consensus algorithm) with failure scenarios and recovery procedures.
Model freshness and consistency SLA specification (maximum update latency, query response time percentiles, consistency model) with measurement and monitoring approach.
Sensor fusion pipeline design (fusion algorithm, outlier detection, confidence scoring, ground-truth calibration procedure) with validation results against reference data.
Query performance optimization design (caching strategy, read replica topology, asynchronous query patterns) with load testing results and latency/throughput projections.

Acceptance test criteria for model completeness (feature coverage checklist, geometric accuracy tolerances, semantic representation rules, deviation/hazard detection sensitivity) with test procedures and pass/fail thresholds.
